steps.name: Wire GET /api/statement end-to-end (AccountUseCase + AccountService + TransactionDto + AccountController)
criteria: AccountUseCase exposes getStatement() returning List<Transaction>
AccountService implements getStatement() — delegates to repository, returns transactions sorted in reverse chronological order
TransactionDto record created in adapter.in.web package with fields: type (String), amount (BigDecimal), date (String)
AccountController exposes GET /api/statement, maps List<Transaction> to List<TransactionDto> — mapping done exclusively in the controller
GET /api/statement returns HTTP 200 with the transaction list (empty array [] when no transactions — never 404)
Instant serialised as ISO 8601 string (spring.jackson.serialization.write-dates-as-timestamps=false)
TransactionDto is never imported by domain or application layers
Walking-skeleton scenario passes with @real-io Spring context
steps.scenario_name: The customer retrieves a non-empty statement after making a deposit
steps.implementation_notes: Extend AccountUseCase interface with List<Transaction> getStatement(). Extend AccountService to implement it by loading all transactions from the repository and sorting by timestamp descending (stable sort — preserve insertion order for equal timestamps). Create TransactionDto as a Java record in adapter.in.web. Extend AccountController with a @GetMapping("/api/statement") endpoint that calls the use case and streams the result through a Transaction→TransactionDto mapper defined inline in the controller. Add spring.jackson.serialization.write-dates-as-timestamps=false to application.properties if not already present.

phases.name: Statement API scenarios — activate and satisfy the 5 @skip scenarios

steps.name: Reverse chronological order — transactions sorted latest-first
criteria: Statement with 3 transactions (DEPOSIT 200, DEPOSIT 100, WITHDRAWAL 50) returns them in reverse order
Position 0: WITHDRAWAL 50.00 | Position 1: DEPOSIT 100.00 | Position 2: DEPOSIT 200.00
AccountService.getStatement() sort is stable — equal timestamps preserve insertion order
@skip tag removed from scenario
steps.scenario_name: The statement shows all transactions in reverse chronological order
steps.implementation_notes: This is the core behavioural validation of AccountService.getStatement(). Verify the sort comparator uses transaction timestamp descending. Remove @skip from this scenario. If 01-01 already produces the correct order, this step is a pure verification — remove @skip and confirm GREEN without additional production changes.

steps.name: JSON field shape — each transaction has type, amount, and date fields
criteria: Each TransactionDto in the response contains the fields: type, amount, date
The date field of the first transaction is a non-empty ISO 8601 string
HTTP 200 returned for a single-transaction account
@skip tag removed from scenario
steps.scenario_name: Each transaction in the statement has the correct JSON fields
steps.implementation_notes: Likely passes after 01-01 once TransactionDto is correctly defined. Remove @skip and run — if GREEN, no production change needed. If RED, inspect TransactionDto field names and Jackson serialisation (record component names map directly to JSON keys; ensure no @JsonProperty renaming conflicts).

steps.name: Empty statement — GET /api/statement returns [] when no transactions
criteria: GET /api/statement on a fresh account returns HTTP 200
Response body is an empty JSON array []
No 404 or 500 returned for the zero-transaction case
@skip tag removed from scenario
steps.scenario_name: The statement returns an empty array when no transactions have occurred
steps.implementation_notes: Likely passes after 01-01 if AccountService returns an empty list and AccountController does not special-case emptiness. Remove @skip and verify GREEN. If RED, ensure the controller does not throw on an empty list and that Jackson serialises an empty List as [] rather than null.

steps.name: Decimal precision — BigDecimal amounts serialised without loss
criteria: DEPOSIT 149.99 and WITHDRAWAL 0.01 are returned with exact decimal values
No floating-point rounding — BigDecimal serialised as JSON number with full precision
Reverse order respected: WITHDRAWAL 0.01 at position 0, DEPOSIT 149.99 at position 1
@skip tag removed from scenario
steps.scenario_name: Decimal transaction amounts are preserved without loss of precision
steps.implementation_notes: TransactionDto.amount is BigDecimal — Jackson serialises BigDecimal as a JSON number preserving scale by default. Remove @skip and verify GREEN. If RED, check whether WRITE_BIGDECIMAL_AS_PLAIN or a custom serialiser is needed to avoid scientific notation (e.g. 1.49990E+2).

steps.name: Balance consistency — statement amounts sum to current balance
criteria: After DEPOSIT 300.00 and WITHDRAWAL 50.00, statement contains 2 transactions
Reverse order: WITHDRAWAL 50.00 at position 0, DEPOSIT 300.00 at position 1
Balance shown in response is 250.00 euros
@skip tag removed from scenario
steps.scenario_name: Statement amounts and current balance are consistent after mixed operations
steps.implementation_notes: IMPORTANT: the step 'the balance shown is {double} euros' is already defined in AccountManagementSteps.java and calls GET /api/balance — NOT GET /api/statement. No envelope DTO required. GET /api/statement REMAINS a plain List<TransactionDto>. This step validates: (a) 2 transactions in the statement with correct reverse order, and (b) that GET /api/balance still returns the correct balance after operations — i.e., Phase 1 retrocompatibility is preserved. Remove @skip only. No production code change expected.

steps.name: Implement StatementView component, getStatement() API call, and App.tsx integration
criteria: bankApi.ts exposes getStatement() calling GET /api/statement and returning the transaction array
StatementView renders transactions in a table with columns: type, amount, date
App.tsx adds a 'Relevé' button toggling boolean state showStatement
When showStatement is true, StatementView is rendered; when false, the main account view is shown
Navigation uses conditional rendering (boolean state) — no react-router dependency added
Empty statement renders an empty table (not an error state)
Component compiles without TypeScript errors
Manual visual verification: deposit, click Relevé, transactions visible in reverse chronological order
steps.implementation_notes: No automated browser AT is specified for this step — validation is manual visual + Vitest/RTL unit tests at the crafter's discretion. StatementView is a pure presentational component receiving the transaction array as props (fetched in App.tsx on button click). Date formatting: display the ISO 8601 string as-is, or apply a locale-aware Date.toLocaleDateString() — crafter decision. Keep App.tsx state minimal: showStatement boolean + statementData array. Fetch on each click (no caching required for phase 2).
